full_text:
Cell

2018 Dec 13;175(7):1972-1988.e16.

doi: 10.1016/j.cell.2018.11.021.

Organoid Modeling of the Tumor Immune Microenvironment

James T Neal et al.,

10ug /ml nivolumab (anti-PD1) treatment for 7 days.

Nivolumab dosage in humans

https://reference.medscape.com/drug/opdivo-nivolumab-999989--> 240-480 mg IV as an adjuvant or 1mg/kg in combination with ipilimumab

50 ug/ ml Nivolumab and 1 ug/ml Pembrolizumab

Pembrolizumab has high affinity to PD-1 compared to all other anti-PD-1 antibodies suggesting the need for lower concentrations / dosage https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4497957/

Anti-PD1 ab in this study is same as ours. Concentration used 10 ug /ml

Pembrolizumab concentration is significantly higher (250 ug/ ml) compared to other studies (10 ug/ ml).

However, they culture the organoids in microfluidics compared to traditional plate format.

According to this table dostarlimab binding affinity is greater than pembro / nivolumab

Ongoing clinical trials with Dostarlimab, chemo, and pembrolizumab in NSCLC

https://clinicaltrials.gov/ct2/show/NCT04581824

Drug: Dostarlimab

Dostarlimab will be administered through a 30 minute infusion at a dose of 500 milligrams (mg) intravenously (IV) every 3 weeks (Q3W) up to a maximum of 35 cycles (each cycle of 21 days).

Drug: Pembrolizumab

Pembrolizumab will be administered through a 30 minute infusion at a dose of 200 mg Q3W up to a maximum of 35 cycles (each cycle of 21 days).

Note: higher dosage of Dostarlimab than Pembrolizumab

1) First check concentrations of the drug in patients: what is the effective dose

https://d201nm4szfwn7c.cloudfront.net/5f95dbd7-245e-4e65-9f36-1a99e28e5bba/3490ed62-a129-4c03-839c-142f331f4cce/3490ed62-a129-4c03-839c-142f331f4cce_viewable_rendition__v.pdf

Here the lowest receptor occupancy (RO) value is 2.44 µg/mL. In patients, however an 8-fold higher was reached with the oral dosing of 1000mg, which is also used in the clinics:

https://us.gsk.com/en-us/media/press-releases/data-from-garnet-study-indicates-robust-activity-of-dostarlimab-in-patients-with-advanced-or-recurrent-endometrial-cancer/

This highest concentration would be 19,52ug/mL with the clinical dosing schedule.

2) Drugs have always a different name prior to clinical registration. For example Dostarlimab is called TSR-042. With that name you will find more preclinical studies such as; https://mct.aacrjournals.org/content/molcanther/early/2019/02/19/1535-7163.MCT-18-0836.full.pdf

In this paper it says TSR-024 had an EC (effective concentration) for suppression of T-cell activity of 133pmol/L.

Here it gets tricky because then you would need to calculate the molecular weight of TSR-042 which I did not find to be publicly available.

Then I found this poster https://www.taconic.com/pdfs/PD1-EORTC-poster-final.pdf

Here the effective dose was above 1000ng/mL so 1ug/mL

So I would say it is safe to start with the 10-100-200ug/mL sheme.

OR we could do a range of 2-20-200 in all the samples? Those would correspond to the minimum dose, clinical dose and then a very high dose.

An estimate of the amount of anti-PD1 needed for GSK project

For concentration test * 3 experiment

Stock anti-PD1 / Dostarlimab concentration = 8 mg/ml

Total volume needed 4.1 ml 650 ul / well *6 (24 well) + 60 *2 (Shandon slide) -> 4 ml+120 ml = 4,1 ml (see below the experiment /plate layout)

Concentrations to test:

2 ug/ml *4.1 ml= 8.2 ug of ab 4099 ul medium + 1.3 ul of Dostarlimab

20 ug/ml*4.ml= 82 ug of ab 4090 ul medium + 10.3 ul of Dostarlimab

200 ug/ml *4.1 ml= 820 ug of ab 3997ul medium + 103 ul of Dostarlimab

Required amount of ab for concentration test / sample = 910 ug* 3 samples = 2730 ug

Required volume of the ab test= 115* 3 experiment = 344 ul

From this test let’s say we choose 20 ug/ml for the experiment

We have 4 conditions to test

Dostarlimab single agent

Dostarlimab + STING agonist

Dostarlimab + PARPi

Dostarlimab + STING agonist + PARPi

Total volume needed = 650 ul / well *6 (24 well) + 60 *2 (Shandon slide) + 150 ul / well *5 (96 well -> 4 ml+130 ml + 800 = 5 ml

Required amount of the ab for 10 samples = 20 ug / ml * 5 ml / condition = 100 ug * 4 condition = 400 ug / sample *10 samples = 4000 ug

Required volume of the antibody for 10 samples = 5 ml / condition *4 = 20 ml--> 50 ul of the ab / sample *10= 500 ul of 8 mg /ml stock ab

Amount of Dostarlimab needed = 2730 ug for concentration test + 4000 ug for 10 samples = 6.73 mg we can request for 8 mg of the antibody
